title: Four parts, progressively darker gray (via different line methods)

rule.instruction: A square divided horizontally and vertically into four equal parts, each with a progressively darker gradation of gray.

restrictions: USER-PICKED instruction (new process: user selects, Claude builds one round then stops). A fresh GENRE after the located-figures streak. Four equal quadrants, each a progressively darker gray. The user's twist: get the difference in gray scale by using DIFFERENT ways to draw lines inside the boxes — not one trick. So tone is built from line METHOD: direction count (single -> hatch -> cross-hatch -> four-way superimposed), line spacing/density, and hand technique, varied across the 16. Monochrome black on white; lines hand-drawn (not-straight) per the governing taste. Density capped so even the darkest box is tone, never a solid ink patch (paper-rip limit). The 16 span the tone MECHANISM (spacing ramp / direction-count ramp / hybrid / mixed-per-box), gutter vs seamless, progression orientation, and hand energy.
